hypothesis: If a cross-modal predictive architecture that trains two modalities to predict the third in latent space (graph/CNN features, RoboCrystallographer text, and simulated XRD patterns) yields lower MAE on Band Gap, Shear Modulus, Bulk Modulus, Dielectric, and Perovskites formation energy than single-modality baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN) on held-out test sets, then cross-modal integration improves material property prediction because joint supervision and contrastive learning produce richer, more generalizable representations.
